similarity_name: Ontology annotation overlap or semantic similarity
definition: Represent each entity by ontology annotations, such as GO terms, pathway terms, phenotype terms, or perturbation classes. Use Jaccard overlap as a lightweight baseline or a semantic-similarity matrix from an ontology package when DAG-aware similarity is available.
example_usage: python set_jaccard_similarity.py --train train_entities.csv --eval eval_entities.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --set-col ontology_terms

citations.title: simona: a comprehensive R package for semantic similarity analysis on bio-ontologies

evidence.summary: The paper describes ontology semantic similarity for genes, biological entities, and groups of terms.
The paper defines group-to-group semantic similarity and implements efficient ontology traversal.
The paper compares annotation-based and topology-based semantic similarity methods for broader ontology use.

citations.title: Metrics for GO based protein semantic similarity: a systematic evaluation

evidence.summary: The paper frames Gene Ontology annotations as a basis for functional comparison of gene products.

limitations: Jaccard overlap ignores ontology depth and parent-child relationships.
Semantic similarity scores depend on ontology version, annotation source, and information-content method.
Ontology annotations can encode study bias and missingness.
quality_gates: Report ontology, namespace, version, annotation source, and similarity method.
Use DAG-aware semantic similarity when ontology hierarchy matters.
Run sensitivity analysis against a simple set-overlap baseline.
